passwordBadge.label: Password protection
passwordBadge.short: Your password never leaves this device.
passwordBadge.details: When you sign in, your device computes a mathematical proof locally. Only that proof is sent to the server — never your actual password.

settingsIndex.title: Settings
settingsIndex.intro: Change how infl0 looks for you and how your timeline gets sorted.
settingsIndex.sortingFeedsHint: These sliders set the global weights for every article. You can also move individual sources up or down on the Sources page (“How much from this source?”) — that adds an extra factor on top.
settingsIndex.trackingIntro: Off by default. You decide whether we may use your reading behaviour for sorting. You can switch it off at any time — no new data will be stored after that.
settingsIndex.trackingLabel: Use reading behaviour for better sorting
settingsIndex.trackingHint: When on, we remember how long you look at the headline, the teaser or the longer text. This helps sorting learn from your reading behaviour.
settingsIndex.onboardingIntro: Show or hide the four welcome cards that introduce navigation, sources, scoring, and themes. They sit at the top of your inflow when on.
settingsIndex.onboardingHint: Off when you have already seen them. Turn it back on at any time to revisit the introduction.

settingsDisplay.heading: Display
settingsDisplay.intro: First choose whether you prefer a light or dark look (or follow your device). Then pick a colour palette. Custom colours stay exactly as you set them.
settingsDisplay.themeHint: Swatches always show the light variant (pastel is softer, warm is richer). Your light/dark setting above applies to the whole app. Below: high contrast or your own colour choices per surface in the text sections further down.
settingsDisplay.appearanceHint: Sets the overall mood for built-in palettes: bright day look, night look, or the same as your phone or computer. Does not change colours you set yourself under “Custom colours”.

auto.label: Same as my device
auto.hint: When you switch the system between light and dark, infl0 follows.

light.label: Always light
light.hint: Keeps the bright variant of your chosen palette.

dark.label: Always dark
dark.hint: Keeps the dark variant of your chosen palette.

pastel_yellow.label: Pastel · yellow
pastel_yellow.hint: Soft pastel look with a yellow tint.

pastel_green.label: Pastel · green
pastel_green.hint: Soft pastel look with a green tint.

pastel_blue.label: Pastel · blue
pastel_blue.hint: Soft pastel look with a blue tint.

pastel_red.label: Pastel · red
pastel_red.hint: Soft pastel look with a red tint.

pastel_purple.label: Pastel · purple
pastel_purple.hint: Soft pastel look with a purple tint.

warm_yellow.label: Warm · yellow
warm_yellow.hint: Warmer tones with a yellow tint.

warm_green.label: Warm · green
warm_green.hint: Warmer tones with a green tint.

warm_blue.label: Warm · blue
warm_blue.hint: Warmer tones with a blue tint.

warm_red.label: Warm · red
warm_red.hint: Warmer tones with a red tint.

warm_purple.label: Warm · purple
warm_purple.hint: Warmer tones with a purple tint.

highContrast.label: High contrast
highContrast.hint: Strong black and white for maximum contrast.

custom.label: Custom colours
custom.hint: You set background and text for each of the three areas (card front, card back, full text) in the matching sections below; other UI is derived from these choices.

settingsDisplay.typographyIntro: Font and text size apply to the card reading areas and full-text view — not menus, meta rows, or other chrome. Adjustable range differs by surface: a tight band on the card front (layout), a moderate band on the back, and a wide band in the reader for comfort. A preview appears while you work in a block or drag the size control.

cardFront.heading: Card · front
cardFront.typographyHint: Typeface and text size for the front of timeline cards (headline and teaser).
cardFront.colorHint: When using custom theme colours, these apply to the card front reading area.

cardBack.heading: Card · back
cardBack.typographyHint: Typeface and text size for the back of a card (longer summary and actions).
cardBack.colorHint: With custom theme colours, these values can also affect other chrome. The “Restore defaults for this area” control above is always drawn with a fixed, readable style so you can use it if the rest of the page becomes hard to read.

reader.heading: Full text
reader.typographyHint: Typeface and text size for the full-article / reader view (e.g. original article in the modal).
reader.colorHint: When using custom theme colours, these apply to the reader body and headings.

customColors.pickerGroupAria: Custom background and text colours for {surface}

settingsDisplay.resetSurfaceAllAria: Reset typeface, size, line spacing, and colours for this area to the app default — {surface}

preview.label: Theme preview
preview.backTeaser: Short teaser shown on the card's back side. What matters here is everyday legibility.
preview.readerBody: This is how a paragraph looks in the full-text reader — enough contrast, calm line height, no distractions.

settingsDisplay.motionHint: Reducing motion helps if gentle transitions feel distracting or uncomfortable for you.

system.label: Match my device (recommended)
system.hint: Follows your operating system's "reduce motion" setting.

reduced.label: Reduced
reduced.hint: Transitions and animations across the app stay short and calm, no matter what your device says.

standard.label: Standard
standard.hint: Full animations. Overrides your device's "reduce motion" setting.

settingsPersonalization.title: Why at the top?
settingsPersonalization.intro: This view explains your current timeline order in plain language. Open an article to see what pushed it up or down. Sorting sliders and per-source weighting work together: the sliders shape every signal globally; “How much from this source?” on Sources adds a separate boost or nudge for that subscription.
settingsPersonalization.algorithmTitle: How your timeline is being sorted right now
settingsPersonalization.algorithmBody: Each article gets points from multiple signals. Newer articles and your reading behavior can raise or lower the result. Sources, categories and tags are balanced so one area does not dominate. The saved score is the weighted sum of the factors below plus explicit per-source weighting from Sources (“How much from this source?”).
settingsPersonalization.engagementIntro: These values summarize what you tended to read longer (or skip) for similar content.
settingsPersonalization.feedsLinkPrefix: Set or change per-source weighting on the

settingsPrivacy.title: Privacy
settingsPrivacy.intro: What we store about you and why. The control — whether we may analyse your reading behaviour — lives in Settings.
settingsPrivacy.philosophyBody: infl0 only stores what it needs: your sources, your sorting preferences and — only if you consent — how long you look at articles. We do not share any of this with third parties and do not use it for advertising. Once you turn the analysis off, no new data is collected.
settingsPrivacy.controlBody: You can change whether we may analyse your reading behaviour at any time in Settings.

settingsTimeline.title: Adjust sorting
settingsTimeline.intro: Use the sliders to decide how strongly each signal influences your timeline. All the way to the left means the signal is ignored; further right means more influence. Changes are saved to your account.
settingsTimeline.formulaHint: Only signals with influence greater than 0 appear here. The same signals are used to sort your timeline.

contentLengthPreference.label: Do you prefer short or long articles?
contentLengthPreference.hint: Left: short articles are preferred. Right: longer articles are preferred. Center: no preference.

published_recency.label: How recently the article was published
published_recency.hint: The newer the article, the higher the value. After about three days the effect is halved.

inserted_recency.label: How new it is on your timeline
inserted_recency.hint: The later it appeared on your timeline, the higher the value.

content_length.label: Take article length into account
content_length.hint: How strongly your preference for short or long articles is used. All the way to the left: length is ignored.

crawl_diversity.label: Variety across sources
crawl_diversity.hint: Coming soon: ensures more variety between sources.

category_diversity.label: Variety across categories
category_diversity.hint: Coming soon: prevents too many similar topics in a row.

tag_diversity.label: Variety across tags
tag_diversity.hint: Coming soon: prevents tags from repeating too often.

engagement_positive.label: What you enjoy reading
engagement_positive.hint: Coming soon: prefers topics you spend more time with.

engagement_negative.label: What you tend to skip
engagement_negative.hint: Pushes articles down if you often skip similar content. Importance sets how much is subtracted.

source_preference.label: Explicit source weighting
source_preference.hint: From Sources → “How much from this source?” Adds preference × a fixed bonus on top of the weighted signals above — not controlled by the sorting sliders.

index.preparingBody: As soon as one of your sources has articles, they will show up here.
index.emptyNoFeedsBody: Add news or blog sources so your timeline can show articles.
index.readerStartBody: {count} new articles since your last reading session.
index.allReadBody: Every article on your timeline is marked as read. Turn on the switch below to show read articles again.

front.desktop: Welcome to your calm news timeline. infl0 turns articles into flip cards: essence on the front, context on the back. Try it now: click the card or use the "E" keyboard shortcut.
front.mobile: Welcome to your calm news timeline. infl0 turns articles into flip cards: essence on the front, context on the back. Try it right away and flip this card once - tap the text, the arrow at the bottom left, or the top-right corner.

back.desktop: Nice, you reached the back side. Use W/S or arrow keys for the next and previous card. Press Q to open the current card's full text.
back.mobile: Nice, you reached the back side. Move to the next card by scrolling down in your timeline. Open this card's longer text with the link at the bottom of the card.

full.desktop:
Shortcut moment: Q opens the selected card's full text and Q or Esc closes it again.

You can find the full shortcut reference on the help page.

Close this view now and jump to the next welcome card with W/S or arrow keys.
full.mobile:
This is the longer card text view. Close it with the close button, then continue to the next welcome card.

When you use infl0 on desktop, you can also use shortcuts like Q and Esc.

You can find the full shortcut reference on the help page.

sources.title: Where the cards come from
sources.front: Your timeline grows with your sources. A few strong RSS or Atom feeds are enough for infl0 to deliver fresh, relevant cards every day.
sources.back: Each source is checked on a schedule, and new entries become cards in your inflow automatically. Start with your favorite feeds, then grow from there.
sources.full:
Sources are your editorial foundation in infl0. You add RSS or Atom feeds, infl0 collects updates in the background, and those updates appear as cards.

Open source management now, add at least one source, then come back to your timeline.

scoring.title: Why this card is on top
scoring.front: You know it as "the algorithm" from TikTok or Twitter. In infl0, the algorithm is there to sort your timeline in a way you can understand and tune.
scoring.back: The score blends freshness, your sliders, and optionally reading behavior. Important: this algorithm is transparent - and you can control it yourself in settings. If you enable tracking, infl0 can usually improve your timeline much more.
scoring.full:
In infl0, "the algorithm" is not a black box: you can see which factors build the score and adjust their weights yourself.

Whenever you save your sorting sliders, your timeline scores are recalculated. That keeps the order aligned with your current preferences.

If you also enable tracking, infl0 can factor in real reading behavior - which often improves personal relevance noticeably.

In "Why at the top?" you can inspect, card by card, which signals pushed ranking up or down.

Open sorting, tracking, or "Why at the top?" now and see the transparency in action.

themes.title: Make infl0 yours
themes.front: Now for your final polish: colors, fonts, and motion controls let infl0 feel truly yours.
themes.back: You can style card front, card back, and full-text reader independently - so every surface stays clear in every lighting condition. On mobile, adjust everything directly in settings. On desktop, you can also use + or - for text size and Shift+K / Shift+L to cycle through typefaces.
themes.full:
In settings you can fine-tune themes, typography, and motion preferences permanently. That keeps infl0 calm, high-contrast, and comfortable whether you skim quickly or read deeply.

When you're ready, close this full text and add your first real source.

feeds.title: Sources
feeds.intro: Add or remove news or blog sources. Your timeline only shows articles from sources you listed here.
feeds.emptyList: No sources saved yet. Add one above.
feeds.openSourceAria: Open source in a new tab: {url}
feeds.copySourceAria: Copy source URL to the clipboard

health.listNote: When we have a status for a source, it appears here. You can use this to see whether a feed is working and how often it updates.
health.noSnapshot: No status yet — we will add one after the first check.

pending.what: We have not fetched this source yet.
pending.action: Nothing to do — we will start at the next scheduled check.

needs_setup.what: We're analysing this source and preparing the first fetch.
needs_setup.action: Nothing for you to do — once we're ready, regular checks continue automatically.

quiet.what: We are checking this source, but it had no new articles recently.

degraded.what: Some articles could not be loaded last time.
degraded.action: Check back in a few hours. If it stays this way, please contact support.

failing.what: The latest fetch did not work. We are still trying.
failing.action: You can pause this source or replace it with another one.

blocked.what: The publisher is blocking us right now.
blocked.action: Nothing to do — we will keep trying later.

paused.action: Resume fetching when you want articles again.

stats.shareTooltip: Percentage of articles in your inflow that come from this source.
stats.readTooltip: How many of this source's inflow articles you've already opened.
stats.noInflow: No articles in your inflow yet.

weighting.label: How much of this source?
weighting.hint: More moves articles from this source up in your inflow. Less moves them down. Effect kicks in on the next refresh.
weighting.hintTriggerAria: How does the source weight work?
weighting.savedToast: Source weighted — takes effect on the next inflow refresh.
weighting.errorToast: Could not save the source weight.

operatorSources.title: Operator · sources
operatorSources.intro: Technical status board across all known crawler sources.
operatorSources.errorLoad: Could not load operator source statuses

register.title: Create account
register.tagline: New here? Get started in under a minute.
register.signInLink: Already have an account? Sign in

episode.playInBrowserHint: Starts the audio file directly in your browser.

article.modalKeyboardHint: Esc closes · use w/s after closing to navigate

help.title: Help & frequently asked questions
help.intro: Short answers in plain language. Expand the details if you'd like to know more.

shortcutsReference.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcutsReference.intro: All keyboard shortcuts in one place. Each row says where it works and what it does. Press the keys without holding Ctrl, Cmd, or Alt — those chords belong to your browser or operating system.
shortcutsReference.scopeIntro: Some keys only do something while a particular surface is on screen — that keeps reading calm and makes sure your taps land where you expect.

scopeRules.noEditable: No shortcut fires while you are typing in a text field, search box, or other input.
scopeRules.noModalBg: While a longer original article or an info popover is open, the timeline and read-toggle keys pause so the article behind doesn't drift away.
scopeRules.selectedCard: Article keys (E, M, Q) only act on the card you're currently looking at — the one that fills most of the screen.
scopeRules.noChords: Holding Ctrl, Cmd, or Alt always wins for the browser. So Cmd+R still reloads the page; it never toggles read articles.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.summary: Move between articles and decide whether already-read items are shown.

article.title: Reading an article
article.summary: Flip the focused card, open the original, or close whatever overlay is on top.

readability.title: Comfort & readability
readability.summary: Nudge text size and typeface for the surface you are looking at — front of card, back of card, or the full-text reader.

navigatePrev.label: Previous article
navigatePrev.description: Scrolls the previous card into focus on your timeline.

navigateNext.label: Next article
navigateNext.description: Scrolls the next card into focus on your timeline.

toggleShowRead.label: Show or hide read articles
toggleShowRead.description: Flips the same switch as the “Show read articles” option in the user menu and the empty-state card.

flipCard.label: Flip the focused card
flipCard.description: Switches between the front of the card (headline + teaser) and the back (longer summary). Only acts on the card you're currently looking at.

toggleReadState.label: Mark read or unread
toggleReadState.description: Marks the focused article as read, or marks it unread again if it is already read.

openOriginal.label: Open the original article
openOriginal.description: Opens the full original text in a reading overlay, when one is available for that article.

closeSurface.label: Close overlay
closeSurface.description: Closes the original article, info popover, or any other overlay on top — and returns focus to whatever was below.

fontBigger.label: Bigger text
fontBigger.description: Grows the text on the surface you're looking at by one step. Stops at the maximum that still fits the card.

fontSmaller.label: Smaller text
fontSmaller.description: Shrinks the text on the surface you're looking at by one step. Stops at the smallest comfortable size.

fontReset.label: Reset text size
fontReset.description: Restores the default text size for that surface (front of card, back, or reader).

fontFamilyPrev.label: Previous typeface
fontFamilyPrev.description: Cycles back through the typefaces available for that surface.

fontFamilyNext.label: Next typeface
fontFamilyNext.description: Cycles forward through the typefaces available for that surface.

passwordSafety.title: How is my password protected?
passwordSafety.simple: Your password never leaves this device. When you sign in, your device computes a mathematical proof and sends only that proof to the server. Even we cannot see your password.
passwordSafety.details: We use the SRP-6a protocol (Secure Remote Password). When you register, your device derives a so-called verifier from your password; only that verifier is stored. When you sign in, your device and the server exchange numbers that let both sides confirm you know your password — without ever transmitting it. In production, the app should additionally be served over HTTPS to encrypt the traffic.

whatWeStore.title: What does infl0 store about me?
whatWeStore.simple: Only the essentials: your email, an optional name, your sources and which articles you've seen. Reading behavior for the ranking is stored only if you explicitly turn it on.
whatWeStore.details: By default, reading-behavior tracking is off (opt-in). When you turn it on, short focus periods (headline, teaser, summary) are recorded to improve your timeline order. You can switch it off anytime in “Privacy” — no new events will be sent after that.

howRanking.title: How is my timeline sorted?
howRanking.simple: Every article gets points. Things like how recent it is and how well it matches your interests matter. Global weights live under “Adjust sorting”. You can also move each source up or down under “Sources” with “How much of this source?”
howRanking.details: Each signal is normalized to a value between 0 and 1 per article. The weighted part of the score is the sum of signal × (weight ÷ 100). Missing data counts as 0. There is a separate slider for article length (shorter ↔ longer). On top of that sum, an optional fixed bonus from per-source weighting is added (set on Sources — not the same as these sliders). Under “Why at the top?” you see every factor, including that row when it applies.

privacyChoices.title: Can I disable personalization?
privacyChoices.simple: Yes. Under “Privacy” you can turn off reading-behavior tracking at any time. Your saved settings and sources stay intact.
privacyChoices.details: When you disable tracking, no new events are sent to the server. Previously computed weightings remain for now. If you want to delete your account entirely, please contact the operators of this instance.

feedsBasics.title: What are sources?
feedsBasics.simple: Sources are news or blog sites that infl0 collects new articles from. You can add or remove them under “Manage sources”.
feedsBasics.details: Technically, these are usually RSS or Atom feeds. If you're not sure whether a site offers one, look for a link labeled “RSS”, “Feed” or “Atom”. The timeline only shows articles from sources you listed here.

shortcuts.title: Are there any keyboard shortcuts?
shortcuts.simple: Yes — there's a full list further up on this page under “Keyboard shortcuts”. The basics: W/S or the arrow keys move between articles, E flips the focused card, Q opens the original article, Escape closes whatever overlay is on top, and R shows or hides articles you've already read.
shortcuts.details: Shortcuts are active in the timeline as long as no input field is focused and no modifier key (Ctrl, Cmd, Alt) is held down. While an original article or info popover is open, W/S, the arrow keys and R pause – the background will not change while you are reading. Escape closes the open surface. The full list, including readability shortcuts (text size and typeface), now lives in the “Keyboard shortcuts” section above.

pwa.description: Calm, privacy-first reading and learning from your personal sources.
pwa.updateReady: A new version of infl0 is ready.
